stories.h: OpenAI opens up its app store
stories.p: Assistants are becoming platforms, and everyone's building on them. Expect a land-grab as brands rush to be where the conversation already is.

stories.h: Anthropic's Claude runs multi-day tasks on its own
stories.p: Agents are quietly moving from demo to day-job. The interesting question is no longer whether they work, but who manages them.

stories.h: Google's Gemini tops every benchmark again
stories.p: The search giant is not losing this race. With its own chips and unmatched distribution, Google keeps turning research wins into product wins.

stories.h: A new open model rivals the giants - for free
stories.p: The gap between open and closed keeps shrinking. For most companies, good-enough-and-free is a very persuasive combination.

stories.h: AI-designed drug reaches human trials
stories.p: This is where AI stops being about productivity. When models start finding medicine, the stakes and the upside change completely.

stories.h: Apple finally ships its AI-native Siri
stories.p: Years late, but the install base is enormous. Overnight, hundreds of millions of people get a capable assistant in their pocket.

stories.h: Meta open-sources a video model anyone can run
stories.p: Hollywood should be paying attention. The tools to make convincing footage are now free and on your laptop.

stories.h: Nvidia's new chip doubles inference speed
stories.p: The moat gets a little deeper. Cheaper inference means AI shows up in places it was too expensive to run before.

stories.h: A startup automates the entire sales call
stories.p: Uncomfortable questions for a lot of teams. The demo is good enough that it's no longer a novelty.

stories.h: The EU agrees the next phase of the AI Act
stories.p: Compliance just became a board topic. Whatever you think of the rules, ignoring them stopped being an option.

stories.h: Microsoft puts an agent in every Office app
stories.p: The assistant era arrives on 400 million desktops at once. The question is how fast people actually change how they work.

stories.h: Amazon's robots now pack orders end-to-end
stories.p: The warehouse is quietly changing. Automation moved from moving boxes to handling the fiddly last step.

stories.h: A coding model passes a senior engineer interview
stories.p: Not a gimmick this time. The transcript is genuinely hard to tell from a strong human candidate.

stories.h: Deepfake fraud hits a major bank
stories.p: The security conversation gets real. Voice and video can no longer be trusted as proof of who is calling.

stories.h: Spotify launches playlists that learn you in real time
stories.p: Music discovery, rewired. The model notices your mood shift before you've finished the first song.

stories.h: Tesla's robotaxi opens to the public in Austin
stories.p: The long-promised ride finally arrives. The next year will decide whether trust follows the technology.

stories.h: An AI tutor outperforms the classroom in a study
stories.p: Education is in for a decade of debate. The result is striking enough that it cannot simply be waved away.

stories.h: Adobe's model edits video by description
stories.p: Type the change and it happens. The line between editing and generating just got a lot blurrier.

stories.h: China's open models top the global leaderboard
stories.p: The race is no longer a one-country story. Cheap, capable and open is a combination that travels fast.

stories.h: A hospital deploys AI triage in the ER
stories.p: Faster answers, new responsibilities. When the model is wrong, who is accountable becomes a real question.
